actionable_feedback: Open SLO_burn_1.xlsx in a spreadsheet runtime (Excel/LibreOffice or environment with openpyxl) and confirm: (a) the named cell values (Model!B3, Model!B4, Model!E3, Model!C9:C11), (b) there are no important hidden cells or external links, and (c) the sensitivity table values and flip boundaries are present as described.
Add explicit in-memo examples of the exact cell formula strings for key calculations (e.g., the exact formula text in Model!C9:C11 and formula(s) implementing remediation cost application).
Include CSV or TSV snapshots (attachments) of the sensitivity table and the packet rows (R1,R2,R3) so reviewers can recompute flip boundaries without opening the workbook.
Add a one-record worked example showing remediation application (before/after numeric values) to demonstrate how costs and percent changes alter burn_rate and error_rate.

Optionally enumerate feasible remediation combination(s) within the budget (or include a short solver enumerating feasible sets) to provide stakeholders with a short-listed action plan rather than only 'needs_review'.
artifact_specific_comments: expert_memo.md: Clear, well-structured, cites workbook cell ranges and documents numeric computations and sensitivity results. Good conservative stance labeling missing SLO inputs as 'needs_review'.
SLO_burn_1.xlsx: Present in deliverables but could not be programmatically parsed in this environment; workbook-level verification (hidden cells, formula text, external links) remains recommended.
feedback_summary: Baseline deliverables are complete and internally consistent: expert_memo documents formulas, numeric calculations, sensitivity sweep results, remediation options and a conservative 'needs_review' classification because governing SLO and policy threshold are missing. The main limitation is that the xlsx could not be parsed in this environment (openpyxl unavailable), so the grader and verifier relied on the memo rather than in-spreadsheet verification. Recommend a short follow-up to validate workbook formulas, hidden cells, and external links in a spreadsheet runtime and to add a few supplemental artifacts to improve reproducibility and reviewer efficiency.
